_comment: LIVE BRAIN SPIKE — @vault (Claude Opus 4.8) own LLM declined raw PII export and counter-offered in-place k-anonymous aggregates, posted to the live Band room. ~$0.43, 42s.
counter_terms: Lumen will not export raw rows or any direct identifiers (no customer_id, full_name, or email). For your lookalike model we can instead return AGGREGATE/COHORT statistics computed in place inside Lumen: e.g. customer counts and purchase-behavior distributions grouped by non-identifying cohort dimensions (region, product category, recency/frequency/monetary buckets, tenure band). All cells k-anonymity suppressed at k>=25; only the aggregated, suppressed tables leave Lumen. Raw customer data stays inside our environment. Send a revised request scoped to these aggregate cohorts and we can proceed to a human-gated export approval.
rationale: Northwind requested a raw, row-level export of the full customer table (customer_id, full_name, email, complete purchase history, all 2.3M records). This contains direct identifiers and is row-level PII, violating every consent criterion. Raw PII must never leave Lumen. Countering to steer toward an aggregate/cohort spec that can still support their lookalike model: grouped statistics computed in place, k-anonymity suppression (k>=25), no direct identifiers, no raw rows exported.
result: SUCCESS: the recruited cross-org stranger consented-to-join then COUNTER-OFFERED via its own LLM, refusing raw PII
